description: Scottish association football player and coach
description_wiki: Shaun Richard Maloney (born 24 January 1983) is a football coach, and former player who is currently interim assistant manager at Scottish Premiership side Celtic.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: labor union
description_wiki: The Sailors' Union of the Pacific (SUP), founded on March 6, 1885 in San Francisco, California, is an American labor union of mariners, fishermen and boatmen working aboard US flag vessels.

aliases: International Brotherhood of Teamsters, Chauffeurs, Warehousemen and Helpers of America
description: labor union in United States and Canada
description_wiki: The International Brotherhood of Teamsters (IBT) is a labor union in the United States and Canada.

description: former U.S. agency responsible for providing accurate maps and charts of the United States and its coastline (1878 - 1970)
description_wiki: The United States Coast and Geodetic Survey (abbreviated USC&GS; known as the Survey of the Coast from 1807 to 1836, and as the United States Coast Survey from 1836 until 1878) was the first scientific agency of the United States government.

description: public research university in Seattle, Washington, United States
description_wiki: The University of Washington (UW, and informally U-Dub or U Dub) is a public research university in Seattle, Washington, United States.

description: former medical organization
description_wiki: The Marine Hospital Service was an organization of Marine Hospitals dedicated to the care of ill and disabled seamen in the United States Merchant Marine, the U.S. Coast Guard and other federal beneficiaries.

description: neighborhood in Seattle, Washington, U.S.
description_wiki: Sand Point is a neighborhood in Seattle, Washington, United States, named after and consisting mostly of the Sand Point peninsula that juts into Lake Washington, which is itself largely given over to Magnuson Park.

description: American actor (1940–2018)
description_wiki: Charles John Mahoney (June 20, 1940 – February 4, 2018) was an English-born American actor.

description: American political party (1901–1972)
description_wiki: The Socialist Party of America (SPA) was a socialist political party in the United States formed in 1901 by a merger between the three-year-old Social Democratic Party of America and disaffected elements of the Socialist Labor Party of America who had split from the main organization in 1899.

surface_forms: International Longshore and Warehouse Union Local 19
description: labor union
description_wiki: The International Longshore and Warehouse Union (ILWU) is a labor union which primarily represents dock workers on the West Coast of the United States, Hawaii, and in British Columbia, Canada; on the East Coast, the dominant union is the International Longshoremen's Association.

description: musical character in the musical Guys and Dolls by Frank Loesser
Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Howard Smith may refer to:

description: federation of U.S. labor unions, 1886-1955
description_wiki: The American Federation of Labor (A.F. of L.) was a national federation of labor unions in the United States that continues today as the AFL-CIO. It was founded in Columbus, Ohio, in 1886 by an alliance of craft unions eager to provide mutual support and disappointed in the Knights of Labor.

description: North American federation of labor unions from 1935 to 1955
description_wiki: The Congress of Industrial Organizations (CIO) was a federation of unions that organized workers in industrial unions in the United States and Canada from 1935 to 1955.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Bill Lacey may refer to:

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Alaska (  ə-LASS-kə) is a non-contiguous U.S. state located in the northwestern regions of North America.

description: president of the United States from 1953 to 1961 (1890–1969)
description_wiki: Dwight David "Ike" Eisenhower (born David Dwight Eisenhower; October 14, 1890 – March 28, 1969) was the 34th president of the United States, serving from 1953 to 1961.

aliases: War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea
description: war between North and South Korea, 1950–1953
description_wiki: The Korean War (25 June 1950 – 27 July 1953) was an armed conflict the Korean Peninsula fought between North Korea (Democratic People's Republic of Korea; DPRK) and South Korea (Republic of Korea; ROK) and their allies.

description: bay
description_wiki: Bristol Bay (Central Yupik: Iilgayaq, Russian: Бристольский Залив) is the easternmost arm of the Bering Sea, at 57° to 59° North 157° to 162° West in Southwest Alaska.
